Package org.mitre.synthea.engine

Class Module

java.lang.Object
org.mitre.synthea.engine.Module
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Cloneable
Direct Known Subclasses:
C19ImmunizationModule, CardiovascularDiseaseModule, EncounterModule, HealthInsuranceModule, LifecycleModule, QualityOfLifeModule, WeightLossModule
public class Module extends Object implements Cloneable, Serializable
Module represents the entry point of a generic module.

The `modules` map is the static list of generic modules. It is loaded once per process, and the list of modules is shared between the generated population. Because we share modules across the population, it is important that States are cloned before they are executed. This keeps the "master" copy of the module clean.

See Also:

    ModuleSupplier allows for lazy loading of Modules.

    • GMF_VERSION

      public static final Double GMF_VERSION
      Version of the Generic Module Framework (GMF).

      public String name
      Name of the module.

      Specialty associated with the module, if any.

    • submodule

      public boolean submodule
      True if this is a submodule, false otherwise.

    • submoduleName

      public String submoduleName
      If a submodule, the original name of this submodule. Otherwise null.

      public Double gmfVersion
      Version of the Generic Module Framework (GMF) used by this module.

      public List<String> remarks

  • Constructor Details

    • Module

      protected Module()
      Protected no-args constructor only allowed to be used by subclasses.

    • Module

      public Module(com.google.gson.JsonObject definition, boolean submodule) throws Exception
      Create a new Module.
      Parameters:
      definition - JSON definition of the module.
      submodule - Whether or not this is a shared or reusable submodule.
      Throws:
      Exception - when an error occurs inflating the module.

  • Method Details

    • getModulePaths

      public static List<Path> getModulePaths() throws URISyntaxException, IOException
      Get the paths to the modules directories, ensuring the right file system support is loaded if we are running from a jar file.
      Returns:
      List of paths to module directories.
      Throws:
      URISyntaxException - if URI syntax is incorrect.
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs.

    • addModules

      public static void addModules(File dir)
      Recursively adds a folder or directory of module files to the static list of modules. This does not need to be executed by the core software. It only is used when the user wants to add another local module folder or during unit tests.
      Parameters:
      dir - - the folder or directory to add.

    • relativePath

      public static String relativePath(Path filePath, Path modulesFolder)
      Create a relative path from a folder to a file, removing the file extension and normalizing path segment separators.
      Parameters:
      filePath - path to a file
      modulesFolder - path to the folder
      Returns:
      relative path to file from the folder

    • loadFile

      public static Module loadFile(Path path, boolean submodule, Properties overrides, boolean localFiles) throws Exception
      Loads the module defined from the file at the given path.
      Parameters:
      path - Path to the module file
      submodule - whether or not this module is a submodule
      overrides - module overrides to apply
      localFiles - true if the file is external to the src/main/resources folder
      Returns:
      the loaded Module
      Throws:
      Exception - when an error occurs loading the module

    • getModuleNames

      public static String[] getModuleNames()
      Unused.
      Returns:
      An array of module names.

    • getModules

      public static List<Module> getModules()
      Get the top-level modules.
      Returns:
      a list of top-level modules. Submodules are not included.

    • getModules

      public static List<Module> getModules(Predicate<String> pathPredicate)
      Get a list of top-level modules including core and submodules.
      Parameters:
      pathPredicate - A predicate to filter a module based on path.
      Returns:
      a list of top-level modules, only including core modules and those allowed by the supplied predicate. Submodules are loaded, but not included.

    • getModuleSuppliers

      public static List<Module.ModuleSupplier> getModuleSuppliers()
      Get the list of ModuleSuppliers.
      Returns:
      a list of ModuleSuppliers. Submodules are included.

    • getModuleSuppliers

      public static List<Module.ModuleSupplier> getModuleSuppliers(Predicate<Module.ModuleSupplier> predicate)
      Get a list of ModuleSuppliers, given a path predicate. No other filtering applies, so this list could include both core and submodules.
      Parameters:
      predicate - A predicate to filter a module based on path.
      Returns:
      A list of ModuleSuppliers.

    • getModuleByPath

      public static Module getModuleByPath(String path)
      Get a module by path.
      Parameters:
      path - : the relative path of the module, without the root or ".json" file extension. For example, "medications/otc_antihistamine" or "appendicitis".
      Returns:
      module : the given module

    • clone

      public Module clone()
      Clone this module. Never provide the original.
      Overrides:
      clone in class Object

    • process

      public boolean process(Person person, long time)
      Process this Module with the given Person at the specified time within the simulation. Processing will complete if the person dies.
      Parameters:
      person - : the person being simulated
      time - : the date within the simulated world
      Returns:
      completed : whether or not this Module completed.

    • process

      public boolean process(Person person, long time, boolean terminateOnDeath)
      Process this Module with the given Person at the specified time within the simulation.
      Parameters:
      person - : the person being simulated
      time - : the date within the simulated world
      terminateOnDeath - : whether or not to terminate if the patient is dead
      Returns:
      completed : whether or not this Module completed.

    • getState

      public State getState(String name)
      Get a state by name.
      Parameters:
      name - - case-sensitive state name.
      Returns:
      State if it exists, otherwise null.

    • getStateNames

      public Collection<String> getStateNames()
      Get a collection of the names of all the states this Module contains.
      Returns:
      set of all state names, or empty set if this is a non-GMF module
